# shimmerkit

> Zero-dependency, framework-agnostic animated loaders & skeleton placeholders. One tuned CSS engine renders pixel-identical output in React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, and plain HTML via Web Components. Accessible by default (role="status", labels, prefers-reduced-motion). Tree-shakeable, tiny, SSR-safe, TypeScript-first.

Use shimmerkit when you need ready-made, accessible loading spinners or content skeletons and don't want to re-tune keyframes, reduced-motion, and screen-reader labels by hand. 19 loader variants + 10 skeleton presets.

## Install

```bash
npm i shimmerkit
```

- React: import from `shimmerkit/react`
- Everything else (Vue/Svelte/Angular/HTML): import `shimmerkit` to register `<shk-loader>` and `<shk-skeleton>` custom elements
- Optional stylesheet for zero-flash SSR: `import "shimmerkit/styles.css"`

## React usage

```tsx
import { Loader, Skeleton } from "shimmerkit/react";

<Loader variant="ring" size={48} color="#6366f1" />
{loading ? <Skeleton variant="card" /> : <Article data={data} />}
```

## Vanilla / Web Components usage

```html
<script type="module">
  import "shimmerkit"; // registers <shk-loader> and <shk-skeleton>
</script>

<shk-loader variant="dots" size="56" color="tomato" speed="1.4"></shk-loader>
<shk-skeleton variant="text" lines="3"></shk-skeleton>
```

The same custom elements work inside Vue, Svelte, and Angular templates.

## Loader API

Component `<Loader>` (React) / element `<shk-loader>` (HTML). Props = attributes.

- `variant`: spinner | dots | bars | pulse | ring | ripple | wave | grid | orbit | bounce | conic | comet | blob | wobble | bouncer | liquid | muncher | wifi | helix (default: spinner)
- `size`: number (px) or CSS length (default: 40)
- `color`: any CSS color (default: currentColor)
- `speed`: number multiplier, 2 = twice as fast (default: 1)
- `thickness`: number (px) or CSS length ‚Äî stroke for ring/spinner/ripple (default: 10% of size)
- `label`: string ‚Äî screen-reader text (default: "Loading")
- `delay`: number (ms) ‚Äî wait before showing, to avoid flashing on fast loads (default: 0)

## Skeleton API

Component `<Skeleton>` (React) / element `<shk-skeleton>` (HTML). Props = attributes.

- `variant`: text | circle | rect | card | avatar | list | table | button | image | grid (default: text)
- `lines`: number ‚Äî lines (text) or rows (table) (default: 3)
- `count`: number ‚Äî items for list/grid (default: 4 list / 6 grid)
- `columns`: number ‚Äî columns for table (default: 3)
- `width`: number (px) or CSS length
- `height`: number (px) or CSS length
- `radius`: number (px) or CSS length (default: 8)
- `speed`: number multiplier (default: 1)
- `delay`: number (ms) ‚Äî wait before showing (default: 0)

## Core (framework-agnostic) API

Importable from `shimmerkit` and safe in Node/SSR/build tooling (no DOM at import):

- `buildLoaderNode(opts)` ‚Üí VNode ‚Äî build a loader vnode tree
- `buildSkeletonNode(opts)` ‚Üí VNode ‚Äî build a skeleton vnode tree
- `nodeToHTML(node)` ‚Üí string ‚Äî serialize a VNode to HTML
- `register()` ‚Äî register custom elements (auto-called in browser)
- `STYLES` ‚Äî the full CSS string
- `LOADER_VARIANTS` ‚Äî readonly array of loader variant names
- Types: `LoaderVariant`, `SkeletonVariant`, `LoaderOptions`, `SkeletonOptions`, `VNode`

## Theming

Everything is driven by `currentColor` plus a few CSS custom properties
(`--shk-size`, `--shk-color`, `--shk-speed`, `--shk-thickness`), so loaders and
skeletons can be themed with plain CSS instead of props.

## Notes

- Accessible by default: loaders use role="status" and an aria-label; honors prefers-reduced-motion.
- No SVG and no JS animation loop ‚Äî pure CSS keyframes.
- License: MIT. Repository: https://github.com/Eswar2000/shimmerkit
